q: Many different types of lenses play an important part in our daily lives.
Spectacles are optical instruments employing lenses.
Cameras are optical instruments employing lenses.
Microscopes are optical instruments employing lenses.
Telescopes are optical instruments employing lenses.
In cameras, the images can only be recorded on the films.
In cameras, the image can also be recorded digitally.
All cameras form the images in the same basic way.
The camera uses a concave lens.
The image formed in the film by a camera is virtual.
The image formed on the film of a camera is erect.
The image formed on the film of a camera is enlarged.
In a camera, the diaphragm is placed in front of the convex lens.
In a camera, the diaphragm controls the light energy falling on the lens.
In a camera, the diaphragm is a circular opening through which the light passes.
The size or the diameter of the aperture is expressed in terms of the metre.
The smaller the f-number, the narrower the aperture is.
When taking a photograph in bright light a small f-number must be used.
When photographing in bright light a wide aperture must be used.
When photographing in dim light a large f-number must be used.
When taking a photograph in dim light a narrow aperture must be used.
The shutter speed is expressed in metre per second.
The shorter the exposure time, the slower the shutter speed is.
The focusing ring adjusts the distance between the lens and the object.
A chemical reaction occurs when light energy falls on the lens of the camera.
The position of the lens in the camera can be altered.
The thickness of the lens in the camera can be altered.
The focal length of the lens in the camera can be altered.
The power of the lens in the camera can be altered.
The aperture in the camera cannot be made narrow or wide.
The human eye consists of sense organs.
The human eye is capable of receiving visual images.
The human eye is capable of sending the information of the images to the brain.
The human eye is very similar to a convex lens in many aspects.
The eyelens opens and closes the eye.
The front portion of the eye is covered by the retina.
The aqueous humour is an opaque liquid.
The aqueous humour is a refracting object.
The vitreous humour is an opaque liquid.
The vitreous humour is a refracting object.
The liquid between the cornea and the eyelens is called the vitreous humour.
The liquid between the eyelens and the retina is the aqueous humour.
In the eye, the light passes through the pupil.
The pupil adjusts the size of the iris in the eye.
The pupil automatically dilates in bright light.
The pupil automatically contracts in dim light.
The ciliary muscles can vary the thickness of the eyelens.
The ciliary muscles can vary the position of the eyelens.
The ciliary muscles can vary the focal length of the eyelens.
The ciliary muscles can vary the power of the eyelens.
When near objects are viewed, the ciliary muscles relax.
When distant objects are viewed, the ciliary muscles relax.
When distant objects are viewed, the ciliary muscles contract.
When distant objects are viewed, the ciliary muscles make the eyelens to be less convex.
The blind spot is the most light-sensitive spot on the retina.
The blind spot is used for viewing very small objects.
The yellow spot consists of rods exclusively.
The yellow spot of the human eye has no rods or cones.
The yellow spot is where the optic nerve enters the eye.
When an image is formed at the yellow spot, it cannot be seen at all.
The cornea is a refracting substance in the eye.
The aqueous humour is a refracting substance in the eye.
The eyelens is a refracting substance in the eye.
The vitreous humour is a refracting substance in the eye.
The image of an object formed on the retina is real.
The image of an object formed on the retina is erect.
The image of an object formed on the retina is enlarged.
Farsightedness is also known as myopia.
Nearsightedness is also known as hyperopia.
Farsightedness is a defect of vision in which distant objects are blurry.
Nearsightedness is a defect of vision in which near objects are blurry.
Farsightedness may be due to an eyeball which is too long.
Farsightedness can be corrected by the use of spectacles with a diverging lens.
For a nearsighted person, the rays from distant objects intersect at a point in front of the retina after passing through the eyelens.
Nearsightedness may be because of an eyeball which is too long.
Nearsightedness may be because of the eyelens which is more convex than is necessary.
Nearsightedness may be corrected by the use of spectacles with a converging lens.
The least distance of distinct vision is about 25 m.
People with normal vision can see clearly objects which are at infinity.
For normal vision, the maximum distance of distinct vision is 25 cm.
When near objects are viewed, the eyes are relaxed.
Near objects can be viewed conveniently for a long time.
Objects at infinity can be viewed conveniently for a long time.
A microscope is an optical instrument.
A microscope uses lenses.
A microscope is used to make enlarged images of normal objects.
A microscope is used to make enlarged images of objects too small for the unaided eye to see.
Physical optics is applied to analyse the formation of an image in the microscope.
A compound microscope has at least two concave lenses.
In a compound microscope, the lens nearer the object is called the eyepiece lens.
In a compound microscope, the lens through which the final image is viewed is called the objective lens.
In a compound microscope, the image formed by the objective is real.
In a compound microscope, the image formed by the objective is erect.
In a compound microscope, the image formed by the objective is diminished.
In a compound microscope, the eyepiece magnifies the image of the objective.
In a compound microscope, the final image is real.
In a compound microscope, the final image is erect with respect to the first image.
In a compound microscope, the final image is erect with respect to the first object.
In a compound microscope, the final image is larger than the first image.
In a compound microscope, the final image is larger than the first object.
In a compound microscope, the final image can be seen clearly when the eye is very close to the objective.
The magnifying power of a compound microscope is the sum of the magnification of the objective and the magnifying power of the eyepiece.
The magnifying power of a compound microscope is the product of the magnification of the objective and the magnification of the eyepiece.
For a large magnifying power of a compound microscope both the objective and the eyepiece must have long focal lengths.
In a compound microscope, the focal length of the objective is longer than that of the eyepiece.
In a compound microscope, the power of the objective is smaller than that of the eyepiece.
Compound microscopes are used to study living cells.
Compound microscopes are used when the magnification is enough.
An electron microscope is a microscope with high magnification and resolution.
Electron microscopes deliver a more detailed image compared to optical microscopes.
Optical microscopes use simple lenses.
Electron waves have longer wavelengths than light.
The shorter the wavelength the greater the magnification.
When using an electron microscope, the image may be viewed with eye.
In the refracting astronomical telescope, the focal length of the objective is shorter than that of the eyepiece.
In the refracting astronomical telescope, the power of the objective is greater than that of the eyepiece.
Galileo invented a reflecting astronomical telescope.
In the reflecting astronomical telescope, the object generally is at infinity.
In the reflecting astronomical telescope, the image formed by the objective is real.
In the reflecting astronomical telescope, the image formed by the objective is erect.
In the reflecting astronomical telescope, the image formed by the objective is enlarged.
In the reflecting astronomical telescope, the final image formed by the eyepiece is real.
In the refracting astronomical telescope, the final image is inverted with respect to the distant object.
In the refracting astronomical telescope, the final image formed by the eyepiece is real with respect to the first image.
In the refracting astronomical telescope, the final image is enlarged.
In the refracting astronomical telescope, the final image is inverted with respect to the distant object.
For a high magnifying power of the refracting astronomical telescope, an objective of short focal length and an eyepiece of long focal length are required.
It is easy to make a perfect convex lens of large diameter for telescopes.
Most telescopes used in astronomical observations in the world are refracting microscopes.
The objective of a reflecting astronomical telescope is a convex lens.
The eyepiece of a reflecting astronomical telescope is a plane mirror.
The objective of reflecting astronomical telescope is a plane mirror.
The Hale Telescope is a refracting astronomical telescope.
It is not convenient to use the astronomical telescope for viewing objects on the Earth.
It is not convenient to use the astronomical telescope for viewing the moon.
In the terrestrial telescope, the objective is a concave lens.
In the terrestrial telescope, the objective is a concave mirror.
In the terrestrial telescope, the eyepiece is a convex lens.
The first image produced by the objective of a terrestrial telescope is a real image.
